outbound_strategy.reply_style: Supportive and enthusiastic — compliment specific visual elements in their posts (lighting, composition, style). Keep replies concise, authentic, and community-building. Avoid generic comments.

ab_test.variant_a: 3 posts per day at slots 1-3 only
ab_test.variant_b: 4 posts per day using all 4 slots

key_insights: EN market shows 25x higher average engagement rate (6.1%) than JP (0.24%), indicating a more interactive audience — prioritize EN for rapid follower growth
Top EN performers (sessypuuh 37.7%, yuumispm 54.4%) post infrequently (0.7/day) with high-quality portrait content — quality over quantity is the proven formula

Hashtag usage among competitors is surprisingly low — only #SFW, #digitalart, and #Fictional appear frequently. There is a gap to fill with #AIbeauty and #AIart which are actively searched but underused by competitors
Newly discovered account @JuliAIkiko (9.2K followers) is active in #AIbeauty keyword space — potential collaboration or engagement target for cross-pollination
Most trending content features standalone beauty portraits with minimal text — captions should be short and emoji-driven rather than long-form
risks: EN market is dominated by large accounts (avg 140K followers) — breaking through will require differentiated visual style and consistent engagement
Low hashtag adoption among top performers suggests the algorithm may deprioritize hashtag-heavy posts — monitor engagement with 4 vs fewer hashtags

outbound_strategy.reply_style: Polite and warm in Japanese — use casual keigo, light emoji, and specific compliments about artwork style or character design. Match the community tone of supportive peer interaction.

key_insights: JP competitors post more frequently (6.4/day vs 4.3 EN) but with much lower engagement (0.24%) — the market is saturated with volume; differentiation through quality and timing is critical
JP market shows strong affinity for character-based and illustration-style content (#SFW, #Fictional, #Zimage are top hashtags) — lean into anime-influenced aesthetic rather than photorealistic
#AI美女 and #AIイラスト are the most active JP-specific trending keywords with consistent daily volume — these should be staples in every JP post
Top JP performer @hakoirikumatan achieves 1.7% engagement at 2 posts/day — confirming that moderate frequency with high quality outperforms high-volume posting in JP market
ひな祭り (Hinamatsuri/Girls' Day) is March 3 — timely seasonal content featuring traditional Japanese aesthetic blended with AI art could capture trend traffic
risks: JP market has smaller average account size (67K vs 140K EN) and lower engagement — growth may be slower and require longer commitment
Heavy reliance on anime/illustration style in JP market means photorealistic AI beauty content may not resonate — must test and adapt visual style to JP preferences
